{{nihongo|'''Kurumano Shrine'''|久留真神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kurumano Shrine'''|久留真神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kurumano Shrine'''|久留真神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kurumano Shrine'''|久留真神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=2025年12月}}
{{神社
| 名称 = 久留真神社
| 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=鈴鹿市}}
| 所在地 = {{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|鈴鹿市|Q207311}}白子1-15-15
| 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
| 祭神 = {{jalink|大己貴尊}}
| 神体 =

| 創建 = 
| 本殿 = 
| 別名 = 
| 札所等 = 
| 例祭 = 4月の第二土・日曜日
| 神事 = 
| 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=久留真神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''久留真神社'''（くるまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|鈴鹿市|Q207311}}白子に{{jalink|鎮座}}する神社である。{{jalink|延喜式内社}}で旧社格は{{jalink|県社}}。通称、'''福徳之宮'''（ふくとくのみや）・'''車之女神'''（くるまのめがみ）<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁 久留真神社|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>。

=== 由緒 ===
創祀は不詳。往昔は、{{jalink|大己貴命}}と{{jalink|須世理姫命}}を通称伊勢の森（現在の白子一帯）に祀ったと伝わる。{{jalink|雄略天皇|Q357235}}の代に、漢織姫命を合祀し福徳の女神と崇め奉った<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁 久留真神社|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>。{{jalink|敏達天皇|Q316637}}の代に{{jalink|淡路国|Q1053717}}の{{jalink|伊勢久留麻神社|Q128866991}}に分祀する<ref>{{Cite web|title=淡路島観光協会|url=https://www.awajishima-kanko.jp/manual/detail.html?bid=374|access-date=2025-12-31}}</ref>。{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}11年、現在の地に{{jalink|遷座}}する。{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}18年4月、{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}から県社に昇格する<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁 久留真神社|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 主祭神：大己貴命
* 配祀神：須世理姫命・漢織姫命<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁 久留真神社|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>

=== 例祭 ===
*4月の第二土・日曜日
*初午祭：3月の第一日曜日
*天王祭：7月第二日曜日
*神事が[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/54 三重県下の特殊神事]に掲載

=== 関連項目 ===
*{{jalink|伊勢国の式内社一覧|Q11378973}}

'''久留真神社'''（くるまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|鈴鹿市|Q207311}}白子に{{jalink|鎮座}}する神社である。{{jalink|延喜式内社}}で旧社格は{{jalink|県社}}。通称、'''福徳之宮'''（ふくとくのみや）・'''車之女神'''（くるまのめがみ）<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁 久留真神社|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>。

=== 由緒 ===
創祀は不詳。往昔は、{{jalink|大己貴命}}と{{jalink|須世理姫命}}を通称伊勢の森（現在の白子一帯）に祀ったと伝わる。{{jalink|雄略天皇|Q357235}}の代に、漢織姫命を合祀し福徳の女神と崇め奉った<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁 久留真神社|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>。{{jalink|敏達天皇|Q316637}}の代に{{jalink|淡路国|Q1053717}}の{{jalink|伊勢久留麻神社|Q128866991}}に分祀する<ref>{{Cite web|title=淡路島観光協会|url=https://www.awajishima-kanko.jp/manual/detail.html?bid=374|access-date=2025-12-31}}</ref>。{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}11年、現在の地に{{jalink|遷座}}する。{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}18年4月、{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}から県社に昇格する<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁 久留真神社|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 主祭神：大己貴命
* 配祀神：須世理姫命・漢織姫命<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁 久留真神社|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>

=== 例祭 ===
*4月の第二土・日曜日
*初午祭：3月の第一日曜日
*天王祭：7月第二日曜日
*神事が[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/54 三重県下の特殊神事]に掲載

=== 関連項目 ===
*{{jalink|伊勢国の式内社一覧|Q11378973}}

{{nihongo|'''Kurumano Shrine'''|久留真神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=2025年12月}}
{{神社
| 名称 = 久留真神社
| 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=鈴鹿市}}
| 所在地 = {{ill|lt=三重県|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=鈴鹿市|Q207311|WD=Q207311}}白子1-15-15
| 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
| 祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=大己貴尊|UNKNOWN}}
| 神体 = 
| 社格 =  {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=式内社|UNKNOWN}}、旧{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=県社|UNKNOWN}}
| 創建 = 
| 本殿 = 
| 別名 = 
| 札所等 = 
| 例祭 = 4月の第二土・日曜日
| 神事 = 
| 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=久留真神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''久留真神社'''（くるまじんじゃ）は、{{ill|lt=三重県|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=鈴鹿市|Q207311|WD=Q207311}}白子に{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=鎮座|UNKNOWN}}する神社である。{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=延喜式内社|UNKNOWN}}で旧社格は{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=県社|UNKNOWN}}。通称、'''福徳之宮'''（ふくとくのみや）・'''車之女神'''（くるまのめがみ）<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁 久留真神社|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>。

=== 由緒 ===
創祀は不詳。往昔は、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=大己貴命|UNKNOWN}}と{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=須世理姫命|UNKNOWN}}を通称伊勢の森（現在の白子一帯）に祀ったと伝わる。{{ill|lt=雄略天皇|Q357235|WD=Q357235}}の代に、漢織姫命を合祀し福徳の女神と崇め奉った<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁 久留真神社|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>。{{ill|lt=敏達天皇|Q316637|WD=Q316637}}の代に{{ill|lt=淡路国|Q1053717|WD=Q1053717}}の{{ill|lt=伊勢久留麻神社|Q128866991|WD=Q128866991}}に分祀する<ref>{{Cite web|title=淡路島観光協会|url=https://www.awajishima-kanko.jp/manual/detail.html?bid=374|access-date=2025-12-31}}</ref>。{{ill|lt=寛永|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}}11年、現在の地に{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=遷座|UNKNOWN}}する。{{ill|lt=昭和|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}18年4月、{{ill|lt=村社|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}から県社に昇格する<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁 久留真神社|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 主祭神：大己貴命
* 配祀神：須世理姫命・漢織姫命<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁 久留真神社|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>

=== 例祭 ===
*4月の第二土・日曜日
*初午祭：3月の第一日曜日
*天王祭：7月第二日曜日
*神事が[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/54 三重県下の特殊神事]に掲載

=== 関連項目 ===
*{{ill|lt=伊勢国の式内社一覧|Q11378973|WD=Q11378973}}

{{出典の明記|date=2025年12月}}
{{神社
| 名称 = 久留真神社

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''久留真神社'''（くるまじんじゃ）は、{{ill|lt=三重県|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=鈴鹿市|Q207311|WD=Q207311}}白子に{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=鎮座|1=UNKNOWN}}する神社である。{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=延喜式内社|1=UNKNOWN}}で旧社格は{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=県社|1=UNKNOWN}}。通称、'''福徳之宮'''（ふくとくのみや）・'''車之女神'''（くるまのめがみ）<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁 久留真神社|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>。

=== 由緒 ===
創祀は不詳。往昔は、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=大己貴命|1=UNKNOWN}}と{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=須世理姫命|1=UNKNOWN}}を通称伊勢の森（現在の白子一帯）に祀ったと伝わる。{{ill|lt=雄略天皇|Q357235|WD=Q357235}}の代に、漢織姫命を合祀し福徳の女神と崇め奉った<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁 久留真神社|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>。{{ill|lt=敏達天皇|Q316637|WD=Q316637}}の代に{{ill|lt=淡路国|Q1053717|WD=Q1053717}}の{{ill|lt=伊勢久留麻神社|Q128866991|WD=Q128866991}}に分祀する<ref>{{Cite web|title=淡路島観光協会|url=https://www.awajishima-kanko.jp/manual/detail.html?bid=374|access-date=2025-12-31}}</ref>。{{ill|lt=寛永|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}}11年、現在の地に{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=遷座|1=UNKNOWN}}する。{{ill|lt=昭和|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}18年4月、{{ill|lt=村社|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}から県社に昇格する<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁 久留真神社|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 主祭神：大己貴命
* 配祀神：須世理姫命・漢織姫命<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁 久留真神社|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>

=== 例祭 ===
*4月の第二土・日曜日
*初午祭：3月の第一日曜日
*天王祭：7月第二日曜日
*神事が[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/54 三重県下の特殊神事]に掲載

{{Shinto shrine | Name = Kuruma Shrine | Image = {{Image requested|cat=Suzuka City}} | Location = [[Mie Prefecture]][[Suzuka City]] Shiroko 1-15-15 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = [[Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]], former [[Prefectural shrine]] | Founded = | Main hall = | Other name = | Pilgrimage sites = | Annual festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Kuruma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kuruma Shrine''' (くるまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Shiroko, [[Suzuka City]], [[Mie Prefecture]]. It is listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] and its former shrine rank was [[Prefectural shrine]]. It is also commonly known as '''Fukutoku no Miya''' (ふくとくのみや) and '''Kuruma no Megami''' (くるまのめがみ)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>.
== History == The date of its original founding is unknown. In ancient times, it is said that [[Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] and [[Suserihime no Mikoto]] were enshrined in what was commonly called Ise no Mori (the present-day Shiroko area). During the reign of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Ayanorihime no Mikoto was additionally enshrined and revered as a goddess of good fortune<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>. During the reign of [[Emperor Bidatsu]], it was divided and enshrined at [[Ise Kuruma Shrine]] in [[Awaji Province]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Awajishima Tourism Association|url=https://www.awajishima-kanko.jp/manual/detail.html?bid=374|access-date=2025-12-31}}</ref>. In Kan'ei 11 (1634), it was relocated to its present location. In April 1943 (Shōwa 18), it was promoted from [[Village shrine]] to Prefectural shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main deity: Ōnamuchi no Mikoto * Associated deities: Suserihime no Mikoto, Ayanorihime no Mikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>
== Annual Festivals == * Second Saturday and Sunday of April * Hatsuuma Festival: First Sunday of March * Tenno Festival: Second Sunday of July * Rituals are listed in [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/54 Special Rituals in Mie Prefecture]

In April 1943 (Shōwa 18), it was promoted from {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} to Prefectural shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main deity: Ōnamuchi no Mikoto * Associated deities: Suserihime no Mikoto, Ayanorihime no Mikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>
== Annual Festivals == * Second Saturday and Sunday of April * Hatsuuma Festival: First Sunday of March * Tenno Festival: Second Sunday of July * Rituals are listed in [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/54 Special Rituals in Mie Prefecture]

revision.text:
<!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Infobox religious building | name = Kurumano Shrine | native_name = 久留真神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Kurumano Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.829463|136.587963}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Kurumano Shrine'''|久留真神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].
{{Shinto shrine | Name = Kuruma Shrine | Image = {{Image requested|cat=Suzuka City}} | Location = [[Mie Prefecture]][[Suzuka City]] Shiroko 1-15-15 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = [[Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]], former [[Prefectural shrine]] | Founded = | Main hall = | Other name = | Pilgrimage sites = | Annual festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Kuruma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kuruma Shrine''' (くるまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Shiroko, [[Suzuka City]], [[Mie Prefecture]]. It is listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|qq=}}. It is also commonly known as '''Fukutoku no Miya''' (ふくとくのみや) and '''Kuruma no Megami''' (くるまのめがみ)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>.
== History == The date of its original founding is unknown. In ancient times, it is said that [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] and [[Suseri-Hime|Suserihime no Mikoto]] were enshrined in what was commonly called Ise no Mori (the present-day Shiroko area). During the reign of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Ayanorihime no Mikoto was additionally enshrined and revered as a goddess of good fortune<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>. During the reign of [[Emperor Bidatsu]], it was divided and enshrined at {{ill|Ise-Kuruma Shrine|WD=Q128866991|lt=Ise-Kuruma Shrine}} in [[Awaji Province]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Awajishima Tourism Association|url=https://www.awajishima-kanko.jp/manual/detail.html?bid=374|access-date=2025-12-31}}</ref>. In Kan'ei 11 (1634), it was relocated to its present location. In April 1943 (Shōwa 18), it was promoted from {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} to Prefectural shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main deity: Ōnamuchi no Mikoto * Associated deities: Suserihime no Mikoto, Ayanorihime no Mikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>
== Annual Festivals == * Second Saturday and Sunday of April * Hatsuuma Festival: First Sunday of March * Tenno Festival: Second Sunday of July * Rituals are listed in [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/54 Special Rituals in Mie Prefecture]
== Related Items == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] * [[List of Shinto shrines]]

revision.text:
<!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Infobox religious building | name = Kurumano Shrine | native_name = 久留真神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Kurumano Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.829463|136.587963}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Kurumano Shrine'''|久留真神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].
{{Shinto shrine | Name = Kuruma Shrine | Image = {{Image requested|cat=Suzuka City}} | Location = [[Mie Prefecture]][[Suzuka City]] Shiroko 1-15-15 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = [[Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]], former [[Prefectural shrine]] | Founded = | Main hall = | Other name = | Pilgrimage sites = | Annual festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Kuruma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kuruma Shrine''' (くるまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Shiroko, [[Suzuka City]], [[Mie Prefecture]]. It is listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|qq=|1=Ken-sha}}. It is also commonly known as '''Fukutoku no Miya''' (ふくとくのみや) and '''Kuruma no Megami''' (くるまのめがみ)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>.
== History == The date of its original founding is unknown. In ancient times, it is said that [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] and [[Suseri-Hime|Suserihime no Mikoto]] were enshrined in what was commonly called Ise no Mori (the present-day Shiroko area). During the reign of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Ayanorihime no Mikoto was additionally enshrined and revered as a goddess of good fortune<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>. During the reign of [[Emperor Bidatsu]], it was divided and enshrined at {{ill|Ise-Kuruma Shrine|WD=Q128866991|lt=Ise-Kuruma Shrine|1=Ise-Kuruma Shrine}} in [[Awaji Province]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Awajishima Tourism Association|url=https://www.awajishima-kanko.jp/manual/detail.html?bid=374|access-date=2025-12-31}}</ref>. In Kan'ei 11 (1634), it was relocated to its present location. In April 1943 (Shōwa 18), it was promoted from {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} to Prefectural shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main deity: Ōnamuchi no Mikoto * Associated deities: Suserihime no Mikoto, Ayanorihime no Mikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>
== Annual Festivals == * Second Saturday and Sunday of April * Hatsuuma Festival: First Sunday of March * Tenno Festival: Second Sunday of July * Rituals are listed in [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/54 Special Rituals in Mie Prefecture]
== Related Items == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] * [[List of Shinto shrines]]
== References == {{Reflist|2}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久留真神社]] <!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Infobox religious building | name = Kurumano Shrine | native_name = 久留真神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Kurumano Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.829463|136.587963}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Kurumano Shrine'''|久留真神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].
{{Shinto shrine | Name = Kuruma Shrine | Image = {{Image requested|cat=Suzuka City}} | Location = [[Mie Prefecture]][[Suzuka City]] Shiroko 1-15-15 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = [[Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]], former [[Prefectural shrine]] | Founded = | Main hall = | Other name = | Pilgrimage sites = | Annual festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Kuruma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kuruma Shrine''' (くるまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Shiroko, [[Suzuka City]], [[Mie Prefecture]]. It is listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|qq=|1=Ken-sha}}. It is also commonly known as '''Fukutoku no Miya''' (ふくとくのみや) and '''Kuruma no Megami''' (くるまのめがみ)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>.
== History == The date of its original founding is unknown. In ancient times, it is said that [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] and [[Suseri-Hime|Suserihime no Mikoto]] were enshrined in what was commonly called Ise no Mori (the present-day Shiroko area). During the reign of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Ayanorihime no Mikoto was additionally enshrined and revered as a goddess of good fortune<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>. During the reign of [[Emperor Bidatsu]], it was divided and enshrined at {{ill|Ise-Kuruma Shrine|WD=Q128866991|lt=Ise-Kuruma Shrine|1=Ise-Kuruma Shrine}} in [[Awaji Province]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Awajishima Tourism Association|url=https://www.awajishima-kanko.jp/manual/detail.html?bid=374|access-date=2025-12-31}}</ref>. In Kan'ei 11 (1634), it was relocated to its present location. In April 1943 (Shōwa 18), it was promoted from {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} to Prefectural shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main deity: Ōnamuchi no Mikoto * Associated deities: Suserihime no Mikoto, Ayanorihime no Mikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>
== Annual Festivals == * Second Saturday and Sunday of April * Hatsuuma Festival: First Sunday of March * Tenno Festival: Second Sunday of July * Rituals are listed in [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/54 Special Rituals in Mie Prefecture]
== Related Items == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] * [[List of Shinto shrines]]
== References == {{Reflist|2}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久留真神社]] <!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Infobox religious building | name = Kurumano Shrine | native_name = 久留真神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Kurumano Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.829463|136.587963}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Kurumano Shrine'''|久留真神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].
{{Shinto shrine | Name = Kuruma Shrine | Image = {{Image requested|cat=Suzuka City}} | Location = [[Mie Prefecture]][[Suzuka City]] Shiroko 1-15-15 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = [[Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]], former [[Prefectural shrine]] | Founded = | Main hall = | Other name = | Pilgrimage sites = | Annual festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Kuruma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kuruma Shrine''' (くるまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Shiroko, [[Suzuka City]], [[Mie Prefecture]]. It is listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|qq=|1=Ken-sha}}. It is also commonly known as '''Fukutoku no Miya''' (ふくとくのみや) and '''Kuruma no Megami''' (くるまのめがみ)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>.
== History == The date of its original founding is unknown. In ancient times, it is said that [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] and [[Suseri-Hime|Suserihime no Mikoto]] were enshrined in what was commonly called Ise no Mori (the present-day Shiroko area). During the reign of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Ayanorihime no Mikoto was additionally enshrined and revered as a goddess of good fortune<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>. During the reign of [[Emperor Bidatsu]], it was divided and enshrined at {{ill|Ise-Kuruma Shrine|WD=Q128866991|lt=Ise-Kuruma Shrine|1=Ise-Kuruma Shrine}} in [[Awaji Province]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Awajishima Tourism Association|url=https://www.awajishima-kanko.jp/manual/detail.html?bid=374|access-date=2025-12-31}}</ref>. In Kan'ei 11 (1634), it was relocated to its present location. In April 1943 (Shōwa 18), it was promoted from {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} to Prefectural shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main deity: Ōnamuchi no Mikoto * Associated deities: Suserihime no Mikoto, Ayanorihime no Mikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>
== Annual Festivals == * Second Saturday and Sunday of April * Hatsuuma Festival: First Sunday of March * Tenno Festival: Second Sunday of July * Rituals are listed in [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/54 Special Rituals in Mie Prefecture]
== Related Items == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] * [[List of Shinto shrines]]
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suzuka City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suzuka City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Mie Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q135039357}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久留真神社]] <!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Infobox religious building | name = Kurumano Shrine | native_name = 久留真神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Kurumano Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.829463|136.587963}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Kurumano Shrine'''|久留真神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].
{{Shinto shrine | Name = Kuruma Shrine | Image = {{Image requested|cat=Suzuka City}} | Location = [[Mie Prefecture]][[Suzuka City]] Shiroko 1-15-15 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = [[Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]], former [[Prefectural shrine]] | Founded = | Main hall = | Other name = | Pilgrimage sites = | Annual festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Kuruma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kuruma Shrine''' (くるまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Shiroko, [[Suzuka City]], [[Mie Prefecture]]. It is listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|qq=|1=Ken-sha}}. It is also commonly known as '''Fukutoku no Miya''' (ふくとくのみや) and '''Kuruma no Megami''' (くるまのめがみ)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>.
== History == The date of its original founding is unknown. In ancient times, it is said that [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] and [[Suseri-Hime|Suserihime no Mikoto]] were enshrined in what was commonly called Ise no Mori (the present-day Shiroko area). During the reign of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Ayanorihime no Mikoto was additionally enshrined and revered as a goddess of good fortune<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>. During the reign of [[Emperor Bidatsu]], it was divided and enshrined at {{ill|Ise-Kuruma Shrine|WD=Q128866991|lt=Ise-Kuruma Shrine|1=Ise-Kuruma Shrine}} in [[Awaji Province]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Awajishima Tourism Association|url=https://www.awajishima-kanko.jp/manual/detail.html?bid=374|access-date=2025-12-31}}</ref>. In Kan'ei 11 (1634), it was relocated to its present location. In April 1943 (Shōwa 18), it was promoted from {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} to Prefectural shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main deity: Ōnamuchi no Mikoto * Associated deities: Suserihime no Mikoto, Ayanorihime no Mikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>
== Annual Festivals == * Second Saturday and Sunday of April * Hatsuuma Festival: First Sunday of March * Tenno Festival: Second Sunday of July * Rituals are listed in [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/54 Special Rituals in Mie Prefecture]
== Related Items == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] * [[List of Shinto shrines]]
== References == {{Reflist|2}}

{{nihongo|'''Kurumano Shrine'''|久留真神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].
{{Shinto shrine | Name = Kuruma Shrine | Image = {{Image requested|cat=Suzuka City}} | Location = [[Mie Prefecture]][[Suzuka City]] Shiroko 1-15-15 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = [[Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]], former [[Prefectural shrine]] | Founded = | Main hall = | Other name = | Pilgrimage sites = | Annual festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Kuruma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kuruma Shrine''' (くるまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Shiroko, [[Suzuka City]], [[Mie Prefecture]]. It is listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|qq=|1=Ken-sha}}. It is also commonly known as '''Fukutoku no Miya''' (ふくとくのみや) and '''Kuruma no Megami''' (くるまのめがみ)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>.
== History == The date of its original founding is unknown. In ancient times, it is said that [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] and [[Suseri-Hime|Suserihime no Mikoto]] were enshrined in what was commonly called Ise no Mori (the present-day Shiroko area). During the reign of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Ayanorihime no Mikoto was additionally enshrined and revered as a goddess of good fortune<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>. During the reign of [[Emperor Bidatsu]], it was divided and enshrined at {{ill|Ise-Kuruma Shrine|WD=Q128866991|lt=Ise-Kuruma Shrine|1=Ise-Kuruma Shrine}} in [[Awaji Province]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Awajishima Tourism Association|url=https://www.awajishima-kanko.jp/manual/detail.html?bid=374|access-date=2025-12-31}}</ref>. In Kan'ei 11 (1634), it was relocated to its present location. In April 1943 (Shōwa 18), it was promoted from {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} to Prefectural shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%b9%85%e7%95%99%e7%9c%9f%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main deity: Ōnamuchi no Mikoto * Associated deities: Suserihime no Mikoto, Ayanorihime no Mikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Office Kuruma Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=2997|access-date=2025-12-30}}</ref>
== Annual Festivals == * Second Saturday and Sunday of April * Hatsuuma Festival: First Sunday of March * Tenno Festival: Second Sunday of July * Rituals are listed in [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/54 Special Rituals in Mie Prefecture]
== Related Items == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] * [[List of Shinto shrines]]
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suzuka City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suzuka City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] {{wikidata link|Q135039357}}
